pattern: AND values can only stay the same or decrease as a subarray grows, so at each right endpoint there are at most O(log max_val) distinct AND values — track them with a compressed map.
hints: For a fixed right endpoint j, the AND of subarrays ending at j takes at most O(log max_val) distinct values as the left endpoint moves left, because each AND operation can only turn off bits.
Maintain a list of (and_value, count) pairs as you extend the right endpoint: merge pairs with the same AND value. Count how many pairs equal k.
When processing nums[j], compute the AND of each previous distinct value with nums[j], then collapse duplicates and add a fresh (nums[j], 1) pair for the subarray starting at j.

edge_inputs.description: all ones, k=1 — every subarray qualifies
k=0 — only subarrays with AND zero qualify
k larger than any element — no subarray qualifies
k not achievable because a required bit is always cleared
AND quickly drops to zero past one element
k=2 from a 3-element subarray but not shorter ones

stress.description: small arrays with small values to keep oracle fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 15) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 15)
    return [nums, k]
